timeline.label: Issued $1.15B zero-coupon convertible notes due 2032; proceeds for power/land, data-center build, share repurchase, and debt repayment
Acquired 271 acres in Austin County, Texas with 285 MW long-term power agreements for future HPC/AI data center campus
Hired Jeffrey Thomas as SVP of AI Data Centers; announced Submer modular AI cooling collaboration
Launched bitcoin treasury / Digital Asset Management (Spot+) strategy using covered calls on held BTC
Bitmain purchase agreements allowing bitcoin-denominated miner payments with repurchase options
Reduced miner useful life from five years to three years, accelerating depreciation on fleet
FY2024 scale-up: average hashrate rose to 27.6 EH/s; mining revenue $379M (+125% YoY)
Griid infrastructure acquisition expanded Georgia footprint (disclosed in FY2025 10-K segment narrative)
Pivoted to large-scale U.S. bitcoin mining after China ban; average hashrate 9.6 EH/s in FY2023
Exited microgrid/solar software legacy; consolidated as pure-play bitcoin miner

industry.structure: Bitcoin mining is a global, commodity-like hash-rate race: four large U.S. operators (including CleanSpark at ~4.3% of network hash) compete on power cost, machine efficiency, and capital access. ASIC supply is concentrated (Bitmain).
industry.share_shift: CleanSpark grew average hash from 9.6 EH/s (FY2023) to 45.6 EH/s (FY2025) but network difficulty rose faster; H1 FY2026 mining revenue fell 7.7% YoY despite scale, signaling hashprice pressure.
industry.trend: Post-2024 halving economics plus rising AI power demand are pushing miners toward HPC hosting pivots and bitcoin treasury strategies; GAAP earnings swing with ASC 350-60 fair-value marks.
